channel.title: Morning Trade
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Trade examines the latest news in global trade politics and policy.

item.title: Streaming tax clouds USMCA review
item.description: Canada’s move to hike fees on U.S. streaming giants risks throwing another wrench into the six-year review of the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement, which formally begins July 1.
item.dc:creator: By Ari Hawkins

item.title: Trump's China summit yields narrow harvest
item.description: President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping agreed to new agricultural purchasing commitments. That falls short of the sweeping reset teased ahead of the summit.
item.dc:creator: By Ari Hawkins

item.title: Trump tariff blow leaves importers in limbo
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariff agenda took another hit last week when a federal court invalidated his replacement duties. Importers still aren’t sure there’s a clear path to relief.
item.dc:creator: By Ari Hawkins

item.title: Tech world calls for narrow 301 tariffs
item.description: Powerful voices in the trade and tech sectors will ask the Trump administration to tread lightly at this week’s hearing on a Section 301 trade investigation into excess capacity.
item.dc:creator: By Ari Hawkins

item.title: Pro-tariff groups up the ante on 301s
item.description: Some steel, solar and manufacturing groups will push for more expansive trade curbs at a hearing this week as part of the Trump administration's Section 301 investigations.
item.dc:creator: By Ari Hawkins

item.title: Refunds start. Scrutiny to follow.
item.description: Trade and customs experts have a message for companies rushing to file refund claims for the roughly $160 billion in tariffs collected illegally over the past year: tread lightly.
item.dc:creator: By Ari Hawkins

item.title: Trump's refund rollout leaves many out for now
item.description: Customs and Border Protection is on track to begin processing its first batch of tariff refunds on April 20. That doesn’t mean companies will see checks anytime soon.
item.dc:creator: By Ari Hawkins

item.title: Industry recalibrates after steel tariff overhaul
item.description: Companies are charting out the next phase of their campaign to influence the Trump administration's steel tariff regime.
item.dc:creator: By Ari Hawkins

item.title: Are the tariffs working?
item.description: It’s been nearly a year since President Donald Trump announced his Liberation Day tariffs. Economists are tracking the short-term impact.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Desrochers
